visual_checks: Look for any form submission and check if the data can be reviewed before final submission
Check if a confirmation step or review page is presented before committing changes
Look for an undo, cancel, or edit mechanism after form submissions
Check if form data is preserved and editable if the user needs to go back and make changes
Look for confirmation dialogs before destructive or irreversible actions
Verify that submitted data can be reversed, checked for errors, or confirmed across all forms
pass_conditions: All form submissions provide a review or confirmation step before final submission
Submitted data can be reversed or undone within a reasonable time period
Data entered by the user is checked for input errors and the user can correct them before submission
A confirmation dialog or summary page allows users to verify their input before committing
Users can edit or cancel submissions before they are finalized
fail_conditions: Form data is submitted immediately with no review, confirmation, or undo mechanism
Irreversible actions occur without a confirmation step
Users cannot go back to review or edit data once they proceed past a form step
No undo or reversal mechanism is available after submission
Data is submitted on a single button click with no way to check entries or confirm intent
na_conditions: Page contains no forms, data submission mechanisms, or user-modifiable content
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Form submission flow could not be tested end-to-end
common_mistakes: Single-step checkout forms that submit payment immediately without a review step
Account deletion or data removal without a confirmation dialog
Multi-step forms that do not allow users to go back and edit previous steps
Profile or settings updates that save immediately without a confirm or undo option
Contact forms that submit on button click with no summary preview of the message

examples.pass: A contact form displays a summary page showing all entered information with 'Edit' and 'Confirm & Send' buttons — the user can review their name, email, and message before final submission, and a confirmation email includes an option to retract the submission within 24 hours
examples.fail: A feedback form submits the response immediately when the user clicks 'Send' with no preview, no confirmation dialog, and no way to edit or undo the submission afterward
examples.partial: The checkout process includes a review step before payment, but the account settings page saves changes immediately on each field blur with no confirmation dialog or undo mechanism

plain_meaning: For pages that require the user to submit any information at all (not just legal/financial), at least one of reversible, checked, or confirmed must be available. This is the Level AAA superset of SC 3.3.4 and applies to a much broader set of forms.
scope_applies_to: Any form that submits user information
Search history, saved items, and bookmark actions
Any submit action where data is stored or acted upon
scope_does_not_apply_to: Pages with no forms and no submission
Legal/financial flows already covered at AA by SC 3.3.4 (those still pass this one if any mechanism exists)
Client-side-only interactions that do not transmit data
Navigation links that do not submit data
borderline_calibration: This is Level AAA and is typically Not Applicable outside AAA reviews.
Any ONE of reversible, checked, or confirmed across the flow satisfies.
Checked (inline validation) is often the easiest satisfier and very common.
Prefer Not Applicable when the review is not targeting AAA.
